predicates.claim: MEG-Phi and EEG-Phi are highly correlated within-subject on paired same-trial recordings
predicates.design: N>=10 healthy adults, simultaneous 306-ch MEG + 64-ch EEG inside MSR, eyes-closed rest x3 + perturbation block
predicates.metric: Pearson r between Phi-MEG and Phi-EEG, paired per-trial
predicates.fail_consequence: Phi is substrate-coupled, revise Phi formalism for modality invariance

predicates.claim: Sub-4ms temporal binning reveals additional differentiation that EEG ceiling cannot capture
predicates.design: Same MEG recording, recompute Phi at 1ms / 2ms / 4ms / 8ms bins
predicates.fail_consequence: Time resolution above 4ms is uninformative for Phi; MEG buys spatial advantage only

predicates.claim: MEG-PCI separates awake-rest vs NREM-N3 at least as well as EEG-PCI Casali 2013 baseline
predicates.design: Awake-rest baseline + sleep MEG (N3 staging via concurrent EEG), TMS-MEG-PCI variant
predicates.fail_consequence: MEG-PCI offers no advantage over EEG-PCI; defer to N-19 EEG-PCI

predicates.claim: Source-space MEG identifies tangential sulcal-wall Phi contributions invisible to 16-ch OpenBCI EEG
predicates.design: Source reconstruction (MNE/dSPM on individual BEM), compare per-source Phi contribution
predicates.metric: Cohen's d between MEG-only Phi sources and EEG-detectable sources
predicates.fail_consequence: MEG and EEG Phi contributions overlap entirely; 16-ch OpenBCI sufficient

predicates.claim: Phi saturates as channel count scales 16 -> 64 -> 306; ceiling reached by <= 64 channels
predicates.design: Three-way comparison on same subjects: 16-ch OpenBCI EEG, 64-ch MEG-cap EEG, 306-ch MEG; subset montage matching
predicates.metric: Asymptotic fit of Phi vs channel count
predicates.fail_consequence: Phi keeps growing through 306-ch; our 16-ch under-samples; need higher-density EEG even without MEG

blockers_to_lock: Korean co-PI required before SNUH IRB protocol can be filed
MEG-compatible TMS coil for MEG-PCI variant
